course_title: Introduction to Psychology: Understanding How People Think, Feel and Act
course_description: This course provides an introduction to psychological science. In this course, you will learn to use psychological concepts, theories and data to understand human behaviour. Topics covered include how people learn, the fallibility of human memory, individual differences in personality and intelligence, motivation, emotion, mental health and well-being, and social and interpersonal processes. You will learn how to collect and interpret psychological data, and how to critically evaluate psychological claims.
timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: The aim of this course is to provide a foundation in psychology, giving students an understanding of the key elements of psychological science.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand key concepts and principles in psychology
Apply psychological concepts and principles to the analysis of human behaviour in real-world settings
Evaluate and explain the validity of research claims in psychology
Communicate psychological concepts, principles and insights to a business audience
Critique methods of collecting and interpreting psychological data

assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Assignments are to be submitted online via Turnitin.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
For full details please review the School of Psychology Student Guidelines - Extensions and Deferred quiz/exam (the web link can be found on the Policies and Procedures tab)
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
